skill.name: fresh-eyes-review
skill.description: Guides a final focused review before commit, pull request, or deployment to catch missed security, logic, and business rule issues.
skill.summary: Run a structured fresh-eyes review before shipping code.
skill.author: 2389-research

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported external command, filesystem, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found only Markdown checklist text and illustrative TypeScript examples. No executable scripts, network access, secret access, prompt injection attempt, or data exfiltration behavior was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Command Execution Detections
low_findings.description: Static detections at the reported command-related lines are false positives caused by Markdown inline code, fenced examples, and checklist prose. The skill does not define executable scripts or instruct use of dynamic shell commands with user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is visible as Markdown documentation and TypeScript example snippets. There is no runnable Ruby, shell command, or skill script in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Filesystem Detection
low_findings.description: The path traversal hit is a security checklist item that tells reviewers to reject or normalize unsafe paths. It is guidance about preventing traversal, not code that reads or writes filesystem paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 42 is a checklist row about path traversal prevention. It does not execute filesystem operations or accept path input.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography And Reconnaissance Detections
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance detections do not correspond to cryptographic code, host inspection, or environment discovery. The cited areas are metadata, examples, or review-process text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive text or a SQL example, not cryptographic functions or system reconnaissance commands. No evidence of malicious intent was found.

content.value_statement: Late bugs often survive tests because the author reads the code with implementation bias. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a focused final review before commit, pull request, or release.
actual_capabilities: Triggers a final review when work is declared done or ready to commit.
Checks touched files for common security vulnerabilities.
Reviews logic, business rules, performance risks, and edge cases.
Prompts immediate fixes, tests, linting, and type checking after issues are found.
Produces a concise completion statement with found and fixed issues.
limitations: It is a checklist skill, not an automated static analyzer.
It depends on the assistant having access to relevant changed files.
It cannot prove that code is defect free.
It does not replace project-specific security review requirements.

use_cases.title: Pre-Commit Quality Gate
use_cases.description: Run a focused review before committing completed implementation work.

use_cases.title: Pull Request Readiness Check
use_cases.description: Find missed logic, security, and business rule issues before opening a pull request.

use_cases.title: Release Fix Sanity Review
use_cases.description: Re-read urgent changes for hidden regressions before deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Finished Change
prompt_templates.prompt: I am done with this change. Use fresh-eyes-review on the touched files before I commit.

prompt_templates.title: Check Pull Request Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this branch with fresh-eyes-review. Focus on security, logic errors, business rules, and performance issues.

prompt_templates.title: Audit A Sensitive Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Use fresh-eyes-review on the authentication changes. Prioritize authorization checks, input validation, error handling, and data leakage.

prompt_templates.title: Review A Release Fix
prompt_templates.prompt: Run fresh-eyes-review on this release fix. Compare the implementation to the intended behavior and identify any missed edge cases.

output_examples.input: I finished the pagination fix. Review before commit.
output: Started a fresh-eyes review of the changed pagination files.
Found one boundary issue in the has-more calculation.
Recommended adding a test for the exact page-size case before committing.

output_examples.input: Check this auth pull request before I open it.
output: Reviewed authorization paths, input validation, and error messages.
Flagged one missing test for expired session handling.

output_examples.input: The hotfix is ready to ship.
output: Reviewed the hotfix for logic, rollback risk, and production edge cases.
Recommended fixing the default and rerunning the targeted test suite.

best_practices: Run it after implementation and tests, but before commit or pull request creation.
Give the assistant the exact changed files or branch diff.
Fix findings immediately and rerun relevant tests before declaring completion.
anti_patterns: Using it as a substitute for tests or code review.
Running it without enough file context to inspect the change.
Ignoring findings because the change already passed tests.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides a final focused review of completed code before commit, pull request, or deployment.

faq.question: Does it run automated security scans?
faq.answer: No. It provides a structured review process that the assistant applies to available code.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it after implementation and tests, before you declare work done or create a pull request.

faq.question: Can it find issues tests miss?
faq.answer: Yes. It targets assumptions, edge cases, and business rules that tests may not cover.

faq.question: Does it modify code automatically?
faq.answer: It asks the assistant to fix discovered issues, but changes depend on your workflow and permissions.

faq.question: Is it only for security review?
faq.answer: No. It also covers logic errors, performance risks, business rules, and release readiness.
